skill.name: flux-kontext
skill.description: Guides image editing with Flux 1 Kontext Pro through the RunComfy CLI, using prompt patterns for precise single-image edits.
skill.summary: Edit one source image with Flux Kontext Pro using targeted RunComfy prompts.
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many Markdown backticks, URLs, path-like examples, and weak-crypto substrings. Most are false positives in documentation, but the skill intentionally invokes a third-party RunComfy CLI/API and handles image URLs, output files, and API tokens. No prompt injection attempt, hidden malware, or unauthorized exfiltration intent was found in SKILL.md.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party CLI and API Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to run the RunComfy CLI, which submits image-edit jobs to an external model API. This is legitimate for the stated purpose, but users should understand that prompts and source image URLs leave the local environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The CLI invocation and outbound API behavior are documented directly. The context supports legitimate image editing, not stealth execution or malicious exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Credential and Generated File Handling
medium_findings.description: The skill documents token storage in a hidden RunComfy config file and generated downloads into an output directory. The described permissions and file size cap reduce risk, but marketplace users should treat the token and generated files as sensitive.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token path, owner-only permissions, download behavior, and size cap are explicitly documented. This is a real operational risk area, but no unsafe token disclosure is shown.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Misclassified as Executable Code
low_findings.description: Most Ruby or shell backtick alerts are Markdown inline code, fenced shell examples, or command names. They are not embedded scripts that execute automatically when the skill is loaded.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The static matches occur inside Markdown formatting and examples. No executable wrapper, script file, or dynamic command construction was found.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal and Weak Crypto Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The path traversal alerts correspond to placeholder image URLs with ellipses, and the weak cryptography alerts match ordinary prose substrings such as description or designed. No actual crypto API or traversal operation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is documentation, placeholder URL syntax, or normal English. I found no file path concatenation, directory traversal logic, or weak cryptographic algorithm usage.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Pattern: runcomfy CLI
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill relies on local CLI commands such as runcomfy run and runcomfy login. This is expected behavior, but it requires a warning because commands can affect local state and send data to a third party.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are clearly present and central to the skill. They use fixed command names and JSON input examples, so command injection evidence is not present.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Pattern: RunComfy Model API and Output Downloads
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill sends requests to RunComfy model endpoints and downloads generated output URLs. This creates normal third-party service exposure for prompts, source image URLs, and generated images.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The external endpoint and download domains are documented directly. The same section limits the endpoints, which lowers maliciousness but confirms network dependence.

dangerous_patterns.title: Filesystem Pattern: Token File and Output Directory
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill references a hidden token file and output directory writes. These are normal for the RunComfy CLI, but they affect local filesystem state.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file paths and storage behavior are explicit. The documented owner-only token file mode and download size cap reduce severity.

content.user_title: Edit Images with Flux Kontext Pro
content.value_statement: Precise image edits often fail when prompts are vague or the wrong model is selected. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Flux Kontext Pro on RunComfy for focused single-image changes.
actual_capabilities: Selects Flux Kontext Pro for precise local edits on one source image.
Provides RunComfy CLI commands for submitting image-edit jobs.
Defines the expected prompt, image URL, aspect ratio, and seed fields.
Gives prompt patterns for preservation, text replacement, and micro-edits.
Explains when to choose related image models instead of Flux Kontext.
limitations: Requires the RunComfy CLI, account access, and valid authentication.
Uses publicly fetchable image URLs for model inputs.
Supports a single source image, not multi-reference workflows.
May drift on compound edits and should use smaller sequential passes.

use_cases.title: Product Label Revision
use_cases.description: Change brand text or color on one product image while preserving lighting and composition.

use_cases.title: Portrait Accessory Edit
use_cases.description: Add or adjust one visible object in a portrait without changing identity or pose.

use_cases.title: Model Routing Guidance
use_cases.description: Decide whether Flux Kontext, Nano Banana Edit, GPT Image 2 edit, or Flux 2 Klein fits a request.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Local Edit
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for one visible change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Flux Kontext Pro to edit this image. Keep the main subject unchanged. Add one small requested change: [describe change].

prompt_templates.title: Preserve Identity
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for people or branded subjects.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Flux Kontext Pro. Keep [identity, pose, framing, clothing, lighting] unchanged. Change only [specific object or detail].

prompt_templates.title: Seeded Variant Series
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate workflow for comparing repeatable results.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run Flux Kontext Pro with this image, prompt, and seed [number]. Produce a focused variant that changes only [detail].

prompt_templates.title: Model Choice Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this image-edit request and choose Flux Kontext only if it is a single-image local edit. Explain any better model choice before running it.

output_examples.input: A portrait needs one new accessory while keeping the person unchanged.
output_examples.output: The assistant chooses Flux Kontext Pro, writes a preservation-led edit prompt, and sends one source image to RunComfy.

output_examples.input: A product photo needs label text changed from one word to another.
output_examples.output: The assistant keeps the bottle, table, and lighting fixed, then asks the model to replace only the label text.

output_examples.input: A user asks for edits across many source images.
output_examples.output: The assistant explains that Flux Kontext is single-image focused and recommends a batch-capable image editing workflow.

best_practices: Start prompts with what must remain unchanged.
Use one source image and one focused edit per run.
Set a seed when comparing repeatable variants.
anti_patterns: Do not combine several unrelated edits in one prompt.
Do not use private image URLs unless RunComfy can access them safely.
Do not use this skill for text-to-image generation without a source image.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It guides Flux Kontext Pro image edits through the RunComfy CLI for precise changes to one source image.

faq.question: Does it generate images from scratch?
faq.answer: No. It is designed for editing an existing source image.

faq.question: Does it support multiple source images?
faq.answer: No. The documented workflow uses one publicly fetchable source image URL.

faq.question: What data goes to RunComfy?
faq.answer: The prompt, image URL, selected options, and generated output are handled by RunComfy services.

faq.question: Is command execution required?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill relies on the local RunComfy CLI to submit and retrieve model jobs.

faq.question: When should another model be used?
faq.answer: Use another workflow for multi-image batches, multilingual embedded text, or new images without a source.
